topic.what: Alignment between privacy policies and actual logging behaviors in Android applications
topic.how: Analyzed 1,000 apps with 86.8M log entries comparing policy statements with runtime logs using LLM-assisted keyword expansion
topic.why_matters: Privacy policies frequently fail to disclose actual data collection via logging creating transparency gaps that undermine user trust and regulatory compliance

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of apps providing privacy policies
Percentage of apps explicitly mentioning logging practices in policies
characteristics.evidence: Text mining of privacy policies with manual verification

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of log-related statements that are simplistic or vague
Percentage of apps with sensitive information leaked in logs
characteristics.evidence: Keyword-based detection combined with LLM-assisted expansion

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of apps with consistent alignment between policies and actual logs
characteristics.evidence: Alignment analysis between policy statements and detected leakage
characteristics.context: Only 4 apps showed consistent alignment out of 1,000

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total number of privacy leakage instances detected
characteristics.context: Most common: GPS coordinates (37,376), IP (11,311), manufacturer/model (10,484)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of leakage cases where info type not mentioned in policy
characteristics.evidence: Cross-reference of leakage types with policy disclosures
characteristics.context: 47,901 of 62,271 leakage instances unstated in policies

novelty: First large-scale empirical study of Android logging practices vs privacy policies
Novel LLM-assisted keyword expansion for sensitive data detection in logs
Comprehensive dataset of 1,000 apps with 86.8M log entries and policy pairs
Multi-dimensional analysis: disclosure prevalence, content characterization, and behavioral alignment
open_problems: Expansion to paid and region-specific applications
Investigation of developer interpretations of regulatory requirements
Development of privacy-aware logging frameworks to minimize leakages
